title: AAR: Team Chemistry System
scope: Built a roster-composition chemistry system across five phases. Players carry stable chemistry attributes (voice/glue/pull/reach) and a permanent flag layer (franchise/big_stage/baggage). Coaches gain three sub-scores and a derived chemistry archetype. Produces four team-level outputs (tone/fabric/drag/tilt) plus a per-game spine pool. Wired into the live game loop and season-end dynasty pass.

rationale.item: Before this work, 'chemistry' lived as a single line: if HC is players_coach, add chemistry_bonus_per_game to home_game_rhythm. A team of strangers and a team of long-tenured veterans got the same chemistry curve as long as they had the same HC archetype.

delegation_events.decision: Don't apply drift per game — append signals to buffer, consolidate at season end to keep season arcs recognizable rather than noisy
delegation_events.quote: Don't apply drift per game. Append signals to a buffer; consolidate at season end. This keeps season arcs recognizable rather than noisy.

delegation_events.decision: Map existing coach classifications to chemistry archetypes via property — no new field, no migration
delegation_events.quote: Existing coach classifications (players_coach, disciplinarian, motivator, scheme_master, gameday_manager, program_changer) map to chemistry archetypes via a property — no new field, no migration.

actions.description: New chemistry module: all composition, drift, flag, spine, pipeline, UI math
Player + Team + TeamChemistryState dataclass; pregame + postgame + adversity hooks in game engine
70 chemistry tests across 6 test files (phases 1-5 + wiring)

risk_posture: The plan called for 'stop at any phase boundary if the feel is wrong' and 'play 5-10 sim seasons' between phases. That validation hasn't happened yet — the work shipped continuously through all five phases on the strength of the unit tests.

validation_claims.claim: Full 60-minute game runs with 34/34 home players getting drama_current set pregame and drift signals emitted postgame, score 45-66, no exceptions
validation_claims.check_method: Run a full game simulation and verify all players have drama_current set and drift signals logged

non_actions.item: Persistence of chemistry attrs across save/load cycles
non_actions.reason: The save path doesn't yet write chemistry back. Drift work is preserved in memory but not across save/load cycles. Adding it is a 20-line patch.
log_game_drift_signals reads mentee_breakout_this_game off the player. Nothing currently sets it.

non_actions.item: Path event detection for coaching_change_survived, mentored_breakout, injury_comeback
non_actions.reason: Each is a single function call to log_path_event but code in the right places to identify them is missing.
chemistry_major_awards is a list on each player; nothing populates it yet. Without it, no one ever earns franchise or big_stage organically.
locker_room_incidents is a counter that nothing increments. The baggage flag has two other paths that do fire from existing dynamics.

non_actions.item: Multi-season dynasty validation: play 5-10 sim seasons to verify career arc distributions
non_actions.reason: The plan called for this validation between phases. The work shipped continuously on the strength of unit tests.

residual_risks: No multi-season sim validation yet; composition curves calibrated against plan exit criteria but not pressure-tested against league-wide season runs
Target distributions not yet measured: ~1-3% franchise, ~5% big_stage, ~3-5% baggage per career
open_hooks: Spine pool creates chemistry lever in adversity moments without affecting normal play
Drift buffer + season-end consolidation pattern enables future longitudinal analysis of player chemistry arcs
chemistry_major_awards list on player is ready to be populated by awards system
section_headers_original: Team object identity inside the engine
unmapped_content: Date: subtitle says 'May 2026' with no specific day; filename says 2026-05-01 not confirmed in document body. Using null. Three commits on claude/plan-team-chemistry-Q3lRn: 7e6e5e8 Phase 1, 8974e76 Phases 2-5, 8390179 live-engine wiring. What Was Hard section: detailed engineering post-mortem on drag formula tuning (glue_total vs mean_glue divisor, calibration targets 1 high-drama→~20 drag, 4 high-drama→~75); voice saturation curve direction bug (glue_shift only applies past effective_voices>2); float rounding in fit_current (abs(0.2 - 0.7) = 0.49999... IEEE 754); team object identity inside engine (e.home_team.chemistry vs user-supplied object). Test coverage table: 15+22+12+10+7+4 = 70 tests with focus areas per file. Plan exit criteria mapped to specific test names. Spine formula verbatim: spine = fabric + (composure − 50) × 0.4 + 10 if any player has voice≥75 AND glue≥75 + 10 × franchise_count.
parser_notes: Date: subtitle 'May 2026' only, no specific day. Filename says 2026-05-01 but per instructions only use date from document body. Using null. 'What Was Hard' section is a distinctive unmapped section type (engineering post-mortem / implementation challenges) that appears in other AARs as well — potential new schema field.
